title: Goal Contracts and Observable Completion · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Turn a vague long-running objective into an executable goal contract. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a measurable goal and done-condition specification after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write a goal as an outcome rather than an unbounded activity.
Attach observable success measures, boundaries, and evidence requirements.
Separate current objective, durable constraints, and optional next work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

from dataclasses import dataclass

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops goal contracts. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Goal Hierarchy trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate mission, milestone, task, and step without losing the terminal outcome.

content.text: For Goal Hierarchy, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Goal Hierarchy precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops goal contracts. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every low-level action deserves its own persistent goal.

stimulus.title: Goal Hierarchy case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops goal contracts. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Every low-level action deserves its own persistent goal.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate mission, milestone, task, and step without losing the terminal outcome. Artifact under review: a measurable goal and done-condition specification.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Goal Hierarchy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Work longer is a valid measurable goal. This fails the Goal Outcome contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Goal Hierarchy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Relate mission, milestone, task, and step without losing the terminal outcome.
